Issue :-

Learn knowledge and art like other nations in progress, if you want to sit like this
Be your own provider, rise up if you want to live with honor in the world
Make yourself useful like a nail in a shoe if you want to be loved by a friend
If you want to see the beloved's face, make yourself a hundred-toothed sword like a dragon
Do not make the path of danger good, fresh, and a threshold if you want to reach the desired destination

1.right:
Pashtun						2						June 1928

If someone wants to increase their shop and business
Then
Advertise in 'Pashtun'

Because

Pashtun is a magazine in the entire Frontier Province -
Pashtun is printed and distributed in thousands -
Pashtun goes to all the big cities -
Pashtun can be read in the Pashto Hujra -
Pashtun is read and heard equally by Pashtun men and women -
Pashtun is the head and the apple of the eye for Pashtuns -
Pashtun follows every Pashtun in every corner of the world -
Pashtun is bought with great love and is not discarded

But

The advertisement must be truthful, meaning as the advertisement is, so should the goods be -
The advertisement should not be against religion or humanity -
The advertisement should not be such that it benefits someone's caste, religion, or nation -
The advertisement can be printed in Pashto -
The advertisement can be translated well and clearly from another language into Pashto -

How

You send your advertisement for your shop, factory, or anything else -
A note will be published for you in Pashto from which the advertisement is sent -
Address: Manager, Advertisements Page, Pashtun Magazine, Peshawar

Pashtun 4 June 1928

The National Love of the King of Afghanistan
(From Maulana Abdul Majeed Afghani)

Among Pashtuns, this proverb is famous: "The year's harvest" is known from spring.
In all the words and deeds of Amir Ghazi, if one reflects and looks at every aspect of his
life, then this matter will become clear to him
that this man was created by God with a special nature and for some special work,
and the love and affection for his nation that is in his heart
he has not acquired through effort, but God has created this quality in his
nature. Neither the ignorance nor the malice of the Pashtuns is capable of him,
that any love and affection acquired through effort should be accompanied by
bad companionship. The individuals of a nation who, for two-two yards of land,
slaughter their own brother and, for the sake of an ordinary profit or loss, destroy each other,
how will love be born in such a nation?
This is also the nation of Amir Sahib, and in his intention, it is certain, but still,
he is mad in the love of his nation, and in "they love them," Allah has such
reached. From him, it is known that he has acquired this national love.

2.right:
June 1928 4 Pashtun
Ghazal by King Ahmad Shah Abdali
My heart, who took it? The mad one with two locks of hair took it.
That's why it became restless, for the calamity of the locks took it.
This is not the calamity of locks, the beloved without calamity took it.
The sun turns towards the face, the heart, a particle of the sun, took it.
If I advised my heart, pain took the counsel from me.
The world's face became a face, the face took the face of the world.
When I was proud of my strength, the heart's strength was plundered.
This book, the river of my tears, water took it with weeping.
Whatever market my heart had, today your beauty stole it.
Beauty was a tribute, no one gave it, it plundered the world.
I said I wouldn't love, but the heart, powerless, took it.
It was not love, but inspiration, you took the peace of my heart.
My heart has no peace, your restless locks took it.

May this entire world rejoice,
Ahmad carried the grief of the beloved.

Pashtun
Peshawar
Volume 1
1928 AD
Corresponding to Dhu al-Hijjah 1346
Number 1

The National Love of the King of Afghanistan

(By Maulana Abdul Majeed Afghani)

Among Pashtuns, this proverb is famous: "A bull is not known by its year of birth."
If someone reflects on all the words and deeds of Amir Ghazi and
looks at every aspect of his life, then this matter will become clear to him
that this man was created by God with a special nature and for some special work,
and the love and affection he has in his heart for his nation
he has not acquired through effort, but God has created this quality in his
nature. Neither the ignorance nor the malice of the Pashtuns is capable of
doing bad things to the love and affection that has been acquired through effort.
The individuals of a nation who, for money and land,
slaughter their own brother and, for the sake of a small profit or loss, destroy everything,
how will love be born in such a nation?
This is also the nation of Amir Sahib, and he knows them well, but still,
he is mad in the love of his nation and has attained the title from Allah (He loves them and they love Him).
From this, it is known that this national love he has acquired

4.right:
Pashtun
6
June 1928

it is not, but he has brought it with him.
* This is also the love that he, alone, among the rebels of Khost,
stood bareheaded and without fear. He told them that the leader of God's
pure self is also with them and will not abandon me.
* And this was also a sign of true love that with these two or three words,
they threw down their tied weapons and pleaded that their fortune and throne
remain stable; they would not do such a thing again.
* This was also the effect of that love when he was in Jalalabad,
taking leave from the border tribes to go to Europe, then the crowd wept,
and this was that beautiful "La Ilaha" (there is no god but Allah) that they were not ashamed of weeping,
as there is no other in their religion. And he told them, "You weep because you
have this idea that someone will kill me.
Look, I am a man like you and your brother. If I
die, then I have many other brothers. Another Pashtun will sit in my place
and you will serve him the same way. But be careful that no stranger comes,
and no one can deny that whoever from the East goes to Europe,
whether poor or a king, he knows everything about his own country.
And the life of Europe is such a spectacle that once someone
enters it, everything else remains outside. Many princes of India
are in Europe. The Kajj-Kulah (Persian hat) of Iran is also a perfume seller in Paris.
is.
Amir Shahid had not gone to Europe; he had only experienced a little of Europe's
charm in Bombay and had forgotten everything else. But Amir
Ghazi made such a visit to that forgetful place that no king of the East
has done so far in the world. But he worried
4.left:
Pashtun
7
June 1928

not a single bit about the sorrow of his nation and homeland, but on every
screen, the sacred map of Afghanistan was presented to him, and it made his heart
tremble from its core.
Saadullah Khan, son of Captain Khansahib, sent a letter from the province.
He writes: -
When Amir Sahib came to Birmingham to see the gun factories,
we Pashtuns also went to welcome him. At the time of the governor's visit,
only very special men were allowed on the station, but we three
were also given permission.
We were standing at the station when there was a commotion, and it became known that Amir
Sahib had arrived by train, and this was the first day that, within five
feet, he stood before our eyes. He boarded that train,
and as the train started moving, the emotional Pashtuns called out loudly,
"May the King of Pashtuns have a good journey!" Amir Sahib immediately put his head
out of the train: -
Amir Sahib: - Do you understand Pashto?
I: - May I be sacrificed for you, we are Pashtuns.
Amir Sahib: - Bring your hand, and with sincerity, he shook hands
and refreshed.
Amir Sahib: - Come to Kabul.
I: - May I be sacrificed for you, very good. We also came from our homeland
here with this thought.
Amir Sahib: - How many other Pashtun boys are in this country?

5.right:
June 1928 AD               8               Pashtun

I - approximately thirty people -
Amir Sahib - Convey my greetings to everyone - How much longer will you
stay here?
I - May I be sacrificed for you, one and a half years -
Amir Sahib - Good, finish your lessons very soon and learn everything
and come to Kabul soon so that all brothers can work together.
It is a pity that I could not see all Pashtun brothers. My
warmest greetings to everyone.
When we were talking with His Highness, it seemed
as if one brother was talking to another brother. We were talking and
people were shouting. Here among the people, it is famous that
there is magic in the smile of the King of England. Whoever he smiles at,
loses their heart.
We talked for ten minutes and Amir Sahib was very
happy with our visit and conversation. When Amir Sahib
left, people kissed our hands and then regularly
greeted us.
From this letter, it can be known how much love for his nation
is in the heart of Amir Ghazi.
The meaning of all these words is that this whole system of the world, which
works with a purpose, and everything sacrifices itself for another, and with this
achievement, it fully runs day and night in the service of another.
And no laziness comes into it. This is due to a special attraction
that God has created in the essence of every particle, and as long as
as these things exist, this attraction will be in them, and they will
5.left:
June 1928 AD 9 Pashtun
is sacrificed -
To this very attraction, in the language of the children of Adam, they call love and affection.
So when in someone's being there is love for their own nation, and in his character
God has created the pain for the homeland, he will in no condition his own
nation not forget and will not be able to forget, and such a person certainly in the world
is successful, and God helps him in every work, and none of his actions
wastes -
So, in the nature of Amir Ghazi is the love for his own nation, and
never will the European agitation and deceptive sellers be able to make him oblivious
from his national interests -
From God, this hope is that he will himself in the progress of the nation
sacrifice or bring his idea to fruition. H.
Either I will lose myself
or I will bring back the lost beloved.
In the previous issue, I had claimed that an article on "Parda"
I would write, but due to some reasons, this article remained for another issue.
Although, in the meantime, a letter from a sister, which in the previous Pashtun
was published, hurts in my heart like a thorn. That
letter, from its heartbreaking words, it is known that our sisters in these
prisons, how much they have been distressed, and these are the dark houses that they
have killed them alive. May God pour water of mercy on the hearts of the Pashtuns of the border
so that their chains may be removed -
And on them too, may the wind of the world blow -
On me, this objection has been raised that Maulvisahib is against the veil...
A. Do you consider that you will also act upon your own statement, or if all this

6.right:
Pashtun June 1928 AD 10

is for others. –
I will also give you the answer to this objection, but only when the Amir
Sahib's tour finishes. He is still seeing Europe.
It is still not well known whether Pashtuns will remain alive
or not. If they remain alive, then we will again think about making sisters alive.
think about. – In the "Future" Pashtun, I will reveal that just as the material people of the world
material people appreciate the Amir Sahib, similarly spiritual people appreciate him
and have their hands in prayer for him. –
In this context, I will write about a revelation that a saint in
Ajmer Sharif was seen in the year 1306. – And what has happened until now
has happened according to that. –

Made a garden on the chest of the flower necklace again.

In the world, the Lord again brought light to the garden | That's why the leaves on the red flowers became green.
Wherever you gaze, there is a garden | Everywhere nightingales are intoxicated, singing again.
A new spirit was born in every person | Every person made the fresh air awake again.
From happiness, every flower split its chest | When it heard the news of the new spring again.
Spring came to the yellow leaves of autumn | Rows of red were formed in the meadow again.
Again, nightingales made an offering of happiness | They don't care if there are rivals in the garden again.
When the breeze intended towards the garden | It made a garden on the chest of the flower necklace again.
The sun-worshipper again became engaged in the worship of the sun | When it saw the strand of the sun's tresses again.
New love was born in the moth | It threw itself again onto the burning ember.
This Hamid did not give an example of himself | because I saw the power of the Creator again.
Mian Abdul Hamid, student of Jamaat-e-Islami Collegiate School ---
Pashtun June 1928 AD 11

Yaghestan and Freedom

Freedom is such a great and good blessing among the blessings of the people that it is always
the foundation of happiness, honor, and progress, and it is always the harbinger of
humiliation, misfortune, helplessness, misery, and destruction. This priceless blessing
is a fundamental stone for the survival and prosperity of every living being. Nature
has no absolute doubt in this belief, if it is not doubted. The power of
survival and existence is entirely in its hands. "Indeed, Allah will not change the condition of a people
until they change what is in themselves." (This means that God does not change the condition of a people unless
they change their own condition themselves.) Despite all these things, still
we see a strange situation in the world. We see that for everything
by doing or not doing which someone suffers harm, a good law
has been made. Preventing harm and protecting rights cause peace
and comfort. Evil and corruption also arise because one human being
encroaches upon the legitimate rights of another human being. If humans, and especially educated
and developed humans, truly ignore someone's property and rights,
then there will be no war and no corruption. Life
of peace is good, not of evil. We do not understand why
Lat Randles is happy about the high villages of Yaghestan and from six
thousand miles away, he has set his eyes on the homes of the poor Yaghestanis.
He says that today or tomorrow we will be forced to our border in
Let's burn Afghanistan: meaning that very soon we will deprive the people of other regions of their own
freedom, we will utterly plunder their homeland and scatter
them. Alas, alas. In the big and high rocks of the mountains, where

6.left:
Pashtun June 1928 AD 11

Yaghestan and Freedom

Freedom is such a great and good blessing among the blessings of the people that it is always
the foundation of happiness, honor, and progress, and it is always the harbinger of
humiliation, misfortune, helplessness, misery, and destruction. This priceless blessing
is a fundamental stone for the survival and prosperity of every living being. Nature
has no absolute doubt in this belief, if it is not doubted. The power of
survival and prosperity is entirely in its hands. "Indeed, Allah does not change the condition of a people
until they change what is in themselves." (This means that God does not change the condition of a people unless
they change their own condition themselves.) Despite all these things, still
we see a strange situation in the world. We see that for everything
that causes harm to someone by doing or not doing it, good laws
have been made. Preventing harm and protecting rights lead to peace
and tranquility. Evil and corruption also arise for this reason: that one human being
encroaches upon the legitimate rights of another human being. If humans, and especially educated
and developed humans, truly turn a blind eye to someone's property and rights,
then there would be no wars anywhere, nor any corruption. All the
evils that arise, their reason is that injustice has been done somewhere.
In the world, the greatest crime and the most severe corruption is to deprive a nation
of its inherent natural right, namely "freedom." This fact
is accepted by everyone. Today, the nations that are free, for their own
natural right "freedom," besides wealth, they have shed their blood like
water. If anyone does not believe, then history is the
If you turn it over, the situation will become known to them by itself. These are not some hypothetical lies.
It is not false, but rather the whole truth, the truth. For example, that to everyone

7.right:
Pashtun 		 12 		 June 1920
His homeland is Kashmir. For every person, their own nest is paradise.
If he can spend his days there in freedom. It is said that one
day a hunter caught a dove, took it home, put it in a beautiful cage,
and kept it with great love, but the dove was not at all
happy and would cry and scream. Finally, the hunter's heart softened,
and he opened the door for it. As soon as the door was opened, the dove flew out.
The hunter also followed. The dove flew and flew until it reached the riverbank.
In the water, there was a piece of wood floating, sometimes submerged and sometimes visible.
The dove went and sat on that piece of wood. When a wave came and
the wood submerged, the dove would fly up, and when the wood reappeared,
it would sit on it. It was in such great difficulty and suffering, but
still, it was happy there. The hunter asked it, "That was a good
mansion, and this is home?" It said nothing, but this message it gave to everyone:
"For everyone, their homeland is Kashmir."
Indeed, for every human, their homeland is beautiful, even if it's a village of mud
or stones. Don't you see that Egypt and many other countries are also
ready to play with their lives for this freedom, so that there may be
justice, justice, then there will be no war and no corruption - life
of peace is good, not of evil. We do not understand why
Lord Randalshaye is happy with the high villages of Yagistan and from six
thousand miles away, he has set his eyes on the homes of the poor rebels.
He says that today or tomorrow we will be forced to impose our border on
Afghanistan: meaning that very soon we will deprive the people of that region of their
freedom, we will plunder their homeland, and we will make them wander from door to door.
Oh, oh. What is in the big and high rocks of the mountains
7.left:
June 1920 		 13 		 Pashtun
that even in the eyes of Lord Randalshaye, such a thought
manifests outward corruption, insecurity, evil, and war. If it's not about war,
then Lord Sahib should know that Pashtun youth are those youth
whose girls speak in songs; they will once sacrifice themselves on the high
rocks of the homeland. And they will show Lord Sahib what it means to give one's
head. But the pity is that Lord Sahib sits in that paradise
island, in a dance hall, and is completely unaware of the wails and cries of the orphans of Kandahar.
He who is eager for war should himself take some
blows to the forehead. Someone truly said that the spectator
hits the middle. What is the benefit of such senseless talk? Unnecessarily bothering people
and inciting them against each other is far from justice. Let's
manage this great world of God with peace and security. For all of us,
it is good enough; everyone's needs are well met in it.
There is no compulsion to create destruction and ruin for oneself,
just as Lord Sahib's thought is. On one hand, the nations of the world are striving
and trying to somehow eradicate the foundation of war. In this wisdom,
as everyone knows, the councils of European nations were held and
will be held. On the other hand, there are also such people who deliberately
disregard the peace and benefit of God's creation,
and to fulfill their own desires, they deliberately seek to create
intensity of evil and opportunity.
In the eyes of wise people, this is a very fallen act. Therefore,
it is necessary to refrain from such actions so that peace
is not disturbed.

8.right:
Pakhtun
             14 June 1928

                   Tax and the Summer Crop
God's and man's wills do not become one. Man's imagination
rides on a horse of conjecture, and God's dealings are based on reality.
As far as human thought goes, it was clear that this year
there would be grain in abundance. The prosperity and growth of the year's crops, outwardly,
was a good tiding of happiness for the poor. Many people were
sitting with the hope that a means for sustenance and peace of mind
would be found. But God did not approve of this. The crooked measure of sustenance
that was destined, was what reached the people. With the ripening of the crop, suddenly
warm happiness turned into sorrow. The poor farmers are not only
sad that all their hard work was carried away by water and for their stomachs too
nothing was achieved. Their greater sorrow and concern is about how
the tax will be paid. There is no doubt that tax is like
the narrowness of the grave; it is difficult to escape and get rid of it, but still, mercy and justice
are considered good things in the world. Mercy is needed for the condition of the poor,
for the destitution of the destitute, for the helplessness of the helpless, for the hardship of the farmers, and then
on such an empty-crop year, consideration is definitely needed. The rightful tax will not
bring any happiness to the farmers. The government should certainly in a good way
ascertain whether the crops were destroyed or empty. If they are truly empty,
then it is necessary that the government does not collect tax from them, because if there is no crop,
then from which side will the tax come? Selling plowing oxen or other farming equipment for tax
should not be done, and if they sell them, then how will they live? In Punjab
In this, there is also this difficulty that the government has shown some kindness, to acknowledge that kindness (editor)
I want to receive from the Pashtun. It is hoped that they will show mercy on their condition.
---
8.left:
June 1928             15                    Pashtun

                 A French sister's letter
                  (The lines below are for reading)
      From English
     - Breakout

I have heard about your educational movement from my
husband several times. And it has become known to me,
that this is a free, awakening, slavery-removing,
national movement. Which teaches the people of this country the lesson of nationalism,
brotherhood, and unity.
Although I am not very familiar with the Pashto language,
still I want to say that I have developed a great connection
with your literary work. I want to subscribe to your
monthly magazine Pashtun. Please accept five rupees as a donation from my side -
Both my husband and I praise your national services.
And we pray very much for your success.

                       Very sincerely
                             yours
                       A French sister
                 We all express our gratitude for this sympathy,
               appreciation, and sincerity of yours.

9.right:
Pashtun 16 June 1928

Zamindar Association Mardan
My Khan! Greetings!
Kindly consider these few lines for the information of common farmers in your magazine
Pashtun. Please print them. We will be grateful.
Due to the problems faced by the farming people residing in Mardan, which they feel,
to get rid of these problems and to find ways for progress and betterment,
on the 10th of May 1928, in a public meeting, an association for farmers
was established under the name "Zamindar Association". The unanimous meeting (President of the meeting)
Khan Sahib Sarfaraz Khan Sahib of Mardan was elected. The purpose of the association
is this: -
A. To maintain good employment among farmers and to
represent the interests of farmers with those officers
with whom farmers have dealings.
B. To find ways for the progress of farmers, the benefit of farming, and the improvement of farmers
under government law, and to
strive for those ways.
9.left:
Yours well-wisher,
Muhammad Ashraf
(Secretary, Zamindar Association, Mardan)
Note:--
How much better it would be if a pure Pashto word were used instead of the word "Association", so that everyone might understand it. 
(Editor)

10.right:
These two, worship and religious morals, I have already left for you.
What action did you take on those that you will take on these? And if a Hindu
claims a hundred thousand in interest from a Khan, then Mullah Sahib will
free him by what Sharia? So now we don't know what the Khan Sahibs will
answer. And if the government, in the service of the educated, makes this call:
"I have already given you reforms in the past, what action have you taken on those
that you now want more? You cannot even manage your own 'bar room'.
There are always Hindu-Muslim disputes in it. You cannot fix your own fees,
that one lawyer demands ten rupees, then another
sends his secretary to you saying, "He will do all your work for five rupees."
And in the end, it's mischief that you will count. The correction of one club is not
done by you. You cannot improve the condition of one Islamic school. There are numerous properties
of endowments, you cannot manage them, so how will you
manage the whole province? This is still little that is given, and Hindus and Muslims are
engaged among themselves, and if more is given, then God knows what
you will do.
Now we are surprised what answer the educated friends will give to these questions.
We are also watching the show, neither among the Khans nor among the educated
are we counted. "A stranger's war is half an Eid." If the Khans win the field,

The 10th Annual Session of the Atmanzai Association "Islah-ul-Afaghina"
Welcome Address
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. We praise Him and send blessings and peace upon His Noble Messenger.
And thereafter. First of all, I extend a heartfelt welcome to my esteemed guests, who have
graciously come to this session from a very long and arduous journey. And
I express gratitude for the favor they have shown to this association. The sympathy they have shown
has visibly increased the morale and courage of the workers and members. May God
give them their rightful reward and recompense. Although the association cannot provide
service befitting the status of its guests, it still hopes that their comfort will not be burdened in any way.
Because this time everyone has their own place. Another thing is that during such a gathering, it is difficult
to maintain complete care. The Islah-ul-Afaghina Association has given you this trouble for the purpose
of presenting to you the entire proceedings, income, expenditure, remaining balance, and educational status of the past year.
Secondly, to make the children of the nation, i.e., the residents of a country,
aware of their national rights. Because the law of nature has instilled in every living being
the love of homeland and the passion for dwelling. It is the nature of every living creature
to be at peace in their own country, even if it is ashes, and in my home, a hut, there is peace and security.
It should be pleasant. For this reason, nature has placed in the inhabitants of every region the power
to protect and care for their homeland, and this is a natural right. The great proof for this statement
is that from ancient times until now, if we observe, it becomes clear that for the
protection and management of a country, there is no need for small or large fortresses, nor is there
good steadfastness in passing an exam in a field, but rather the need for a few brave qualities,
which are the attribute of courage and a center, meaning gathering around one leader.
.

11.right:
Pashtun 20 June 1930

The history of the pre-Islamic era of the Arabs shows us that compared to the world,
this is a small peninsula and its inhabitants were people of caves or deserts who had no
regular government possible among them. Only every tribe had a leader. The
civil wars in their own homes had no limit or measure. But still, the emperors of
Iran and the Caesars of Rome had not made them subjects. Because of this, despite all this,
the seal of love for the homeland was imprinted on their hearts and they had a center of connection which was
the respect for the House of God (Baitullah). Everyone believed that my life
and wealth should be sacrificed for the honor of the House of God. Whenever there was an attack from outside, then with a simple signal
all tribes would unite and they would be united against the foreign power. Furthermore, in the present time
we are surrounded by those who remain free. Those centers of learning and universities do not exist among them
that would ignite the fire of freedom on this side. Most of them are in their own national power system
not dependent on any other nation. This is only because they are free and their own homeland
managing it themselves is the natural and inherent right of the inhabitants of every homeland. This is not a gift
nor is it distinguished; rather, a few natural traits are necessary, which are human, bravery, and
gathering in one place. As long as these original traits of a nation are changed, then their
own rights are also lost and ruined. God Almighty has shown a clear rule:
Indeed, Allah will not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves - It is never the law of the Almighty
that He changes the state of honor or the qualities of progress of a nation into a state of bad condition or humiliation.
Until a nation itself has changed its qualities of courage, manliness, and justice into useless qualities
through oppression. And they are not negligent of their good and natural rights. Islam and
the religion of Islam and the teachings of Islam have been given to the world by the Almighty
so that man may firmly establish his natural traits in it and the human
natural rights that have been snatched from them and taken by force
He takes it from him again.

June 1930 22 Pashtun

Pharaoh had destroyed all the rights, honor, and respect of the Children of Israel. Prophet Moses,
upon our Prophet and him be peace and blessings, after the office of Prophethood, brought this message: "AN
MA'ANA BANI ISRA'IL." Meaning, my first demand from you is the freedom of the Children of Israel, remove the chains of
captivity from them, and return to them the rights that you have snatched from them.
This summary sketch of Islam that the Quran shows us is the true nature.
FITRATALLAHI ALLATI FATARANNASA 'ALAIHA is a witness to this. All humans possess original innate qualities.
This is the result of Islamic education, and this is the intent. However, whenever the natural
dispositions of a nation cannot remain correct on their own, for this reason, the need for Islamic education arises.
When the original dispositions of a nation are corrupted and their natural rights are
lost, the reason for this is wrong upbringing, that is, living under the shadow of a foreign nation.
The Holy Prophet (PBUH) has stated in his blessed words: "MA MIN MAWLUDIN YULADU ILLA
'ALAL FITRATI FA-ABAWAHU YUHAWWIDANIHI WA YUNASSIRANIHI WA YUMAJJISANIHI KAMA YUNTAJUL BAHIMATU BAHIMATAN JAM'A'A HAL
TAJIDUNA FIHA JAD'A'A. HATTA TAKUNU ANTUM TAJDA'UNUHA." Every child is born free from the mother.
The yoke of slavery of no power is around his neck. This is "true nature." But
if the mother and father have him raised at the hands of a foreign nation, under the influence of Judaism, Christianity,
or Zoroastrianism, then because of this, their natural qualities
change from bravery, manliness, and justice into evil qualities. He remains fearful and helpless
from demanding his own essential and natural rights. The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave a clear example
that a healthy calf is born from a cow or buffalo, which has neither a missing ear, nor a nose,
nor a head. It is completely perfect. But you yourselves [corrupt] your own children.

I seek refuge in Allah, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing, the All-Seeing, from the accursed Satan.
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
All praise is due to Allah, the Most High, the Most Great, the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing, and blessings and peace be upon His Messenger sent to the black
and the red. As for what follows! The service of leadership of Islam and the dear nation which
you elders have entrusted to me, although I do not see this capability in myself, yet
for leadership, such a personality is needed who possesses many, many perfections,
and in me, there are so many weaknesses that might not be in anyone else. But since

beneficial for the people. And from you
I have this hope that

Justice of Islam | Islam has given equal status to all mankind.
It has made sacred legal rights equal for all. Of course, Islam is of the
opinion that every person should be moral and pious, and he should be highly respected,
as "Inna akramakum indallahi atqakum" (the most pious is the closest to God)
is a witness. The Holy Prophet (PBUH) has said that Arabs have no superiority over non-Arabs, and non-Arabs
have no superiority over Arabs merely because of being Arab or non-Arab.
Rather, the consideration is for piety, because Hazrat Bilal (RA) was an Abyssinian slave whom
Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (RA) bought and freed. Hazrat Umar Farooq (RA) used to
call Hazrat Bilal (RA) his master. In the same way, Hazrat Salman (RA), Hazrat Hudhayfah (RA), Hazrat Suhayb (RA)
were also slaves and were counted among the great noble companions; this is because of piety.
Islam has established such unity to this extent that in one incident, Hazrat
Umar, during his caliphate, traveled from Medina Munawara to Bayt al-Maqdis alone
traveling, and a slave was with him. Farooq-e-Azam (RA) and the slave took turns riding.
When they reached near Bayt al-Maqdis, the slave was riding and Farooq-e-Azam said that no, the turn for riding
is yours. At that time, people in groups had come out to see the glory and greatness of the Caliph of the time
and for the reception - and Hazrat Hudhayfah was holding the reins in his hand.

The Necessity of Tabligh | Of the brothers, of the elders of the religion, of the nation – Today, Islam will be
subjected to many baseless objections, and in the eyes of every nation, Islam appears in a
bloody form. What is the reason for this? Elders, when I carefully
consider, I come to the conclusion that either there is absolutely no tabligh among us, or if
it is done, it is done in such a way that it brings no benefit other than harm.
From this reason, non-Muslim nations have not seen the bright form of Islam, and this
is the matter that until a religion is preached in its original form,
its good and bad aspects cannot be known. For this, if
we want these bad objections to be removed from Islam, then we must
come out into the field with sincerity and show the true face of Islam to the world.
But today, those nations are very active in spreading their religion, even though they
have no prescribed method of worship for non-nations in their religion. In
reality, tabligh is the right of Muslims. The Gracious God says concerning tabligh: O Messenger!
Convey what has been revealed to you from your Lord. And if you do not, then you have not conveyed His message. O Messenger, convey
that which has been sent down to you from your Lord, and if you do not do so,
then you have not fulfilled your duty. And there are other verses of this kind in the Holy Quran
present. But due to length, I have not quoted them. If tabligh were not necessary,
then Allah Almighty would not have commanded His beloved to do so.
And in the noble Hadith, the accepted Messenger says: Convey from me, even if it is a single verse.
Convey to the people from my side, even if it is a single verse. From this, it is known
that the greatest work of the Messenger in the world was tabligh, and of all the Messengers,
if we look, they endured thousands of hardships, injuries, and heat,
and sacrificed their lives for the religion, but they did not turn away from tabligh.

the customary ritual, that girl with gold was thrown in, and the river began to rise
and fall. And the people of Egypt came to Amr ibn al-Aas with a complaint. So Amr ibn al-Aas
was astonished. So he wrote the whole incident to Hazrat Umar (may Allah be pleased with him). So
he wrote a few words in reply and commanded that these words be thrown into the river.
These words are:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. To the Nile of Egypt, from Abdullah Umar ibn al-Khattab, Amir
al-Mu'minin (Commander of the Faithful): If you flow by yourself, then we have no need of you, but if you flow by Allah, then flow
in the name of Allah. - Translation: I begin in the name of God, who is Rahman and Rahim.
Written to the Nile River of Egypt, from Abdullah, whose name is Umar ibn
al-Khattab. So from him, the meaning is that if you flow by your own power,
then we have no need of you, and if you flow by the command of God Almighty,
then flow in the name of God.
So when those written words were thrown into the river by Amr ibn al-Aas,
the river water rose sixteen cubits - and then it never rose to that extent again.
it did not rise.
Allahu Akbar. This is the result of pure, wise people, how much the Pure Lord
has bestowed honor.
O God, protect all of us Muslims, especially our Pashtun brothers, from these
customs and traditions. -Amen-

Abdur Rab Khan Sahib Fakhr, B.A., LL.B. (Advocate, Peshawar)

When the British Empire was first established in this region or the border area,
the government began a settlement in this area. First, a preliminary settlement
took place, then a legal settlement in the years 70-74, and then another settlement in 96-97
took place. In these settlements, besides information regarding lands, one task was also
undertaken by the officials, which was to record the customs of all tribes, meaning what they are bound by in inheritance and other
matters. Unfortunately, it was recorded for all Pashtun women that
they do not accept their half-share according to Sharia, either by custom or by one another.
Not only was this written in the custom books, but after that,
it came to millions of acres of Pashtun areas, and in it, repeatedly, the Pashtun
language expressed these words (God forbid). In this matter,
the injustice they have done to themselves and the punishment that God
has given to the Pashtun nation for it, the thought of it makes one's body shiver.
Among the great works that Islam has done in the world,
one major achievement is that before the arrival of Islam, great injustice was done to women.
Women were treated like other property, goods, cows, and buffaloes, and were not even considered their own property.
They were not given the status of a human being, but Islam, for the betterment of women
and to give them the status of a human being, enforced many laws, and because of that,
it happened that this woman was also counted among human beings.
You think about the fact that in the Quran, God Almighty says: "Hunna libasun"
"lakum wa antum libasun lahunna." Women are your garment, and you are the garment of women.
is. It is as if your veil is on another address. God has given equal status to women
is. Similarly, regarding the matter of inheritance, He says: "To the male, the equivalent of the portion of two females" ---

Pashtun 50 June 1929 AD

Announcement
The famous Shah Abdul Rahman Sahib of Kat has
fixed a prize of fifty rupees for the best page - the life
account of the page should not be less than two pages and not more than five pages -
Relevant points are required in it - Three judges will make the decision - The essay
must be sent to our office by August 15 - The result will be in September
published in the Pashtun magazine -
Mian Ahmad Shah Barrister-at-Law - Post Office Peshawar

A few words for essay writers
(1) Essays should be in clear, simple Pashto
(2) The words of the essay should be in authentic Pashto - Bazaar or vulgar language
is not required -
(3) Special care must be taken regarding religion, ethics, and civilization
(4) Essays that defame or corrupt habits are not required
(5) Instructive stories and proverbs that teach the nation should be written
in beautiful phrasing -
(6) For the script, learn the style from "Pashtun" -
Attention to feminine gender is necessary as much as possible -
Essays should be written on one side of the paper and separately
(7) The essay must reach by the 10th of every month
and if any essay is not published, it cannot be returned -
(8) The editor has full authority to shorten or lengthen the essay if he helps ---

Method of Education

There are different schools for education. The education of these schools
is divided into three parts. Each part has a separate name.
The first part is called (Ibtidaiya). In this, there are five classes.
The second part is called (Rushdiya). In this, there are four classes.
And the third part is called (Aliya). This is equivalent to (B.A.).

35.right:
January 1929 18
Pashtun

Between the current situation and Western (European) progress, as they say "the middle path is the best," I seek that middle ground which is free from the harms of both our situation and the West. It is my desire that we initiate what is acceptable for our religion and our country. And for those of our educated ones whose natural inclination is toward other nations, it is appropriate for them to use their intellect alongside others, so that we may be saved from the air and the flood that has inclined the nature of our people away from the traditions of their own country toward foreign ways.

Today we consider it the progress of Pashto to educate our sisters. But I ask my nation: if foreign influence increases further—and it is necessary that it will increase, because if a boy gets an education and breathes the air of Europe's free temperament, it cannot be expected that this uneducated girl will walk in step with him. The reality is that if you put different things in one place, it is impossible for them to work together. So, in such a situation, I am surprised: if these shame-filled things remain in our homes, what will we do with them, and what will be the final result? It is necessary for us that we ---
19 January 1929
Pashtun
Either stop the boys' education, or make arrangements so that they are equal.

Another point is our current situation. If a war, which is inevitably coming, begins, what will we do then? Will we save ourselves, or will we abandon these fields? It is not right to abandon this true Pashto and follow hidden principles. We should provide the rights and training that the Prophet (PBUH) established, and save ourselves from the destruction that is coming. It is necessary for us to start in such a way that tomorrow no one points fingers at us, and it becomes a cause of our progress even in times of peace. Our (bad customs) regarding the women of the house, the health of the children, and their upbringing are all in the hands of Pashto. The woman is responsible for everything. The first school for a child is the mother's lap. So, if the woman is not educated, everything will be ruined, and the nation will be further destroyed.

(In the meantime, the article by Jafar Shah Sahib is also pending. It is necessary for our article writer to do more. The purpose of the meeting each day was fulfilled. Now the friendship with Florinli would be completed, and this gathering of happiness would end in the evening. Saeed was saying in his heart every hour with wonder, "O God, will I also bear the separation from Zarqoon along with other troubles?" He was busy in thought when Florinli said, "Look, it is in the Alhambra." Saeed raised his eyes and looked. Allahu Akbar! What a grand and magnificent fortress it is! It seems exactly as if its high walls are touching the sky, and the towers reaching the sky were visible in the distance. On the floor of the earth, the red and white stones look strange; you would say that different flowers are scattered in the garden of paradise. I stood at the Alhambra, examining the graves of those who had scattered.
The cool air passes underneath; he would look at every wall with a gaze of admonition, and he would call out to the people who used to live in the palace.
So I said, "O Alhambra, is there a return?" It replied, "And can those who have died return?"
Then, will these people of the Alhambra return again? It said ---
Those who die in the dust, when do they return? Alas, time destroys everything, alas!
When I heard this, I looked at their memorials and trembled. It is a pity; what benefit did my weeping and lamenting bring? The traces of those who have passed are reminders that recall the scattered.
The memorials of the people who have passed are like weeping roots, weeping over them. Saeed now reached a courtyard whose floor was made of marble. On all four sides of the courtyard, there were rows of tall, tall rooms, whose high roofs were talking to the sky. Saeed thought that in some era these were places of residence, or he looked at his own poverty, and tears flowed from his eyes involuntarily. He wanted to weep in this way and vent the steam of his heart, but Florinli's presence did not consider that weeping appropriate. A little while later, seeing that Florinli was very busy looking at the designs and decorations of the building, he went secretly to the opposite room so that he could sit in that room and reflect on his fate. Whenever he reached the door, his eyes fell on the writing that was inscribed on the door, and when he read it, the sound of "Ya Abata" (O my dear father) came from his mouth, and he fell unconscious to the ground. 65.right:
January 1929

In this meeting, with the participation of Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, Abdul Akbar Khan, Rustam Khan, and other Pashtuns, this Jirga began in the month of November. In this meeting, it was decided to form the Pashtun "Zalmo Jirga" (Youth Jirga), similar to the "All India Youth League" group in India. The basic purpose of forming this Jirga was to show the Pashtuns the path of national unity, independence, and the struggle for their rights. In this meeting, the Pashtun leaders emphasized in their speeches that Pashtuns must adopt the path of unity and strive for a common goal. The leaders of this Jirga told the participants of the meeting that without unity, Pashtuns cannot achieve their rights and for this purpose, they must support each other in all circumstances. Likewise, decisions were also made in this meeting to strengthen the cultural and social affairs of the Pashtuns. It is worth mentioning that the message of unity was spread in the meetings of this Jirga and it guided the Pashtuns to rise as a united force. In the course of the work of this Jirga, the need for increased efforts for unity and consensus among the youth in different regions of Pashtunkhwa was mentioned. ---

68.right:
On January 25, there was a meeting in a large congregation in Tank, attended by people and important Khans of the area. In the meeting, we conveyed the message of brotherhood to all. With sincerity, we met Mukartiya and Azizullah. On January 12, in a meeting in Lachi, people gathered, and sincere workers from Kohat also took the trouble to come. I reached Peshawar for prayer and supplication.

On December 12, I set out again, and toured the areas of Badragh, Ero, Rani Zane, Kot, Miana, Kot, and Spin Kharo, Alandi Valley. There is no doubt that the people are ready, and some workers have a pick-up shop, offering it in Badragh. In Miana, due to the workers and Abd al-Latif Sahib Effendi and his like-minded associates, an independent Islamic school is also running. The members of this school, from these landlords
68.left:
January 1923

33

And the road began, the journey ended on the nimble donkeys, and it was a long trek, always continuing, over the Emuno, and then through very difficult Pashtun terrain. Besides this, we could not escape the continuous rain.

Although the Miana area is mountainous, the mountains are still inhabited, and the land is suitable for cultivation, with all provisions prepared. There is also much hunting. Pheasants, partridges, and partridges are plentiful, and the people of this area subsist entirely on this. For a long time, the government has been aware of these people, and there is no doubt about it. For a single instance, five rupees in fines are levied on many people. If one wishes to hunt, there is no prohibition. But if one wishes to hunt on their own land, and permission is not granted by Lachi, there is no way they can be deprived of their rights to the land, and outsiders hunt freely. And upon these poor Pashtuns

70.right:
January 1920

I say amen to this statement of my Pashtun brothers of the American nation, who give a lot of money in the path of noble service and love!

The truth is that today, work cannot be done without money. You see, today, any nation that does not have a strong treasury cannot have new propaganda, hospitals, and schools for its nation, and cannot serve the country and religion of its nation; and if a nation does not have a treasury, then you see that it is equal to nothing else, which is the state of the nation.

If a person asks why Pashtuns do not do as other nations do, this statement is wrong; Pashtuns give much more charity than their means. But their method of charity is such that it is not of immediate benefit to the nation, it is not eternal; undoubtedly, their method of charity is in the form of the past era, but today its benefit is a loss to the nation.

The era has changed; it was necessary for Muslims to have changed as well. But they did not change with the era, because instead of benefit, it is a loss for them. In the past, people would serve the nation and religion for free, and you would offer money for people, and they would give it to us; in this way, the service of the nation continued, and the servants were also able to make a living.

But you see today that everything is for money. The era has changed; propaganda, publication, school, imamate—in fact, there is no matter that can be done without money, and such a person will not die who girds his loins to serve the nation for the sake of God. If a person girds his loins, then if he is wealthy, like our Pashtuns, he will be able to make a living, but if he is poor, then what?

For this reason, today it is necessary for Muslims to move with the pace of the era, for Pashtuns to form a national treasury with unity like other nations for money, goods, and cash ideas, and to help the nation, and to remove the neediness of the nation, and to support the servants of Islam. Because I hope that our Pashtun brothers will imitate the Pashtun brothers of America and will walk in their footsteps.

The day that our American brothers' charity and alms were placed by the association for the education of the group, because today the most necessary thing for us is to acquire knowledge, the receipt is below:

“Look here! We have identified twenty-seven individuals – from the district – and several of them committed grievous wrongs against the poor villagers. It was, after all, a matter of this: you are not well, sir – you must speak the truth, you will be astonished – if it does not work, you were unaware of the facts – so, O Pashtun people, what was our fault? We have twice as many.
The truth is that these outsiders do not desire this – that we Pashtuns also harbor this sentiment – that the government grants rights to the deserving and injustice to the undeserving. These outsiders say, ‘Whatever the decree, the people should place their trust in it.’ You will say, ‘What are the people discussing today? A servant goes to them and says, ‘Come, come!’ Times have changed – you will see – today the power and authority of the kingdom in England rests in the hands of the workers – that is, the authority of the empire is held by the laborers – but this is the account of a past event – that a group of people in the Great Assembly (Parliament) of England have proposed a series of measures, granting some rights to the laborers.  I do not ask you – what should be done with him, what he did yesterday in the name of the laborers and their rights.  Yesterday, the right to rule became theirs.
Pashtun people, the world changes, and it has changed – therefore, it is not just us who have experienced this; the daughter of Abbas Khan, and the interior of Abbas Khan, where we are entangled in rituals, hair, and wealth – the old order has been abolished – it was necessary that Abbas Khan should abandon the jinn and the wealth for the sake of Abbas Khan – let him run, and leave his people and his possessions – and if he does not, then he will perish.
After the month of Ra, we brought Tul to Charsdeh Tehsil – and the government is in disarray.
When it was evening, I inquired about the situation of the doctor – I asked, ‘No one has brought him from the village.’  Mr. C. – what did we do at that time?  What a day it has been! We also had the seventh arrangement – it was closed – I was anxious to know why it had been closed – either Mia Mala or my own servant came – and it was discovered that some police officers had permitted entry on foot – I am grateful for their assistance on such a day – I thought, ‘Perhaps the police officers would have appealed to Makkah Sahib’ – he is a persistent man – pay their salary late – I am growing weary – what more, my friend – but I worked well with them and removed all their documents – I answered, ‘I am not hungry for bribes – I will see – will you listen to me again?’ and I closed my mouth – I saw – the next day, Wali Sahib came. I asked them – what was the reason for closing it?  What was the answer?  That day, when Abbas Khan was released, the situation had arrived. ”
78.left: When it became evening, I asked my friend about the situation—I asked—why did you not bring the food from the village, it became a matter of honor, that Mr. Willy I.C.S., who at that time had helped the youth—and some days have passed. We also had a seventh arrangement—it has been closed—I was surprised—that finally for what reason would it have been closed—Mian Malaya himself came—and it became known—that some of the police officers had given permission for the band to enter on foot—I was surprised by their action that day, and I am grateful for their sympathy—I thought—that perhaps the police officers might have complained to the Sahib—that Sahib, this is a continuous man—the youth's salary is small, it is difficult to make ends meet—he is a brotherly friend—but why did I also do a good job with them—and I fulfilled all their visas—I gave him an answer—that I do not eat the hunger of a bribe—look—that you will hear from me again—and I closed the door—what do I see—that the next day Willy Sahib came—I asked him—what is the reason for closing their door—he gave an answer—that the day I released Abbas Khan, he had come to see the situation.

79.right:
Pashtun
February 1930

Congress Session in Lahore

The Congress is an assembly of the Hindus, Muslims, Sikhs, Parsis, and Christians of India. Once a year, this session is held in one of the provinces of India, and people from all over India gather there. The work of this assembly is to liberate our country (India) from foreigners. These people strive day and night, thinking of ways to free ourselves from the slavery of foreigners.

This year's session of this assembly was in Lahore. Many foreigners from America, Germany, and England came to observe it. It is a pity that although this session came so close to our frontier, very few people from Pashtun participated.

What should I mention about this session, and in what detail should I describe it? If I were to tell you everything, this entire magazine of mine would be filled, and there would be no room left for other articles. Even then, the pleasure and understanding that I experienced would not be fully conveyed. Therefore, I wish to present to you only the necessary points of this session.

You must be aware that many efforts are being made by this assembly. The British have given us some rights, but since this is our country, it is necessary that we have some share in the government. ---

The Pashtun
February 1930

Let me turn to the situation in Peshawar - there, when it was burning, it was still closed.

One day I was sitting - what do I see, a soldier came and said: "Come inside - the Sahib Bahadur has a telegram for you." The Sahib was from Bareilly, who is now the General of our province's doctors and prisons, and he was very angry - the Sahib was very furious - he said, "I have troubled many people, come to my bungalow." I replied that I am forbidden - if I come tomorrow, I will not be able to return.

I said to him, "What is my fault in this? You are troubled by people outside, and you have locked my door from the inside."

Therefore, there is no answer to the question, nor any consideration - do whatever you want, take me away.

Although I have heard that the General Sahib is very happy with people visiting his bungalow - but my bad luck, Abdul Ghaffar.

Pashto literature, from the pen of a Pashtun, has brought existence and speech to life like stillness and like itself; freedom, the noise of freedom all around is a favor to the workers, this is the strength of the white youth of London - you see nothing, as if there is no one to share the grief, even if there is a jewel, if it is not seen, we are not companions because we are slaves, in the sight of taking deep thought, it is not like this upon a Pashtun, this education brought us to jail in India.

Pashto and the evening

In what expediency and blessing, in sweet Pashto, whether it is known to anyone or not, everyone admits that for the last hundred years we have been responsible for our own ruin - because through the path of customs, charities, and beautiful traditions, Pashtuns have been thrown into very bad hardships - and the anger is that, until now, fifty Khans, we are neither aware nor of our own deeds, nor are there good and sweet people in the nation who would consider the state of their nation - and forbid them from wrong paths - there are many such wise people among the Pashtuns - who are attached to their own religion - but still, people do not consider those religions as a refuge, and in any case, want to do good - the result is that, until now, the nation, if not for benefit, is causing harm to itself - here it is mentioned that other Pashtuns are becoming aware of fifty Khans because of the Pashtun magazine - if you have read the news of America's charity in the Robanji magazine - Praise be to God that the same action has started in the week - the Pashtun brother Mufid Khan Sahib of North Khattak, five rupees donation for the Shabqadar night charity, defense of the earthquake, and this will be considered a good thing - if we mention the mental box of the Pashtun's imaginary mention, that he also joined in such deceptions with himself -

84.right:
Pashtun - February 1920

The Deeds of Truth's Death

Truth is bitter, if it is a true matter, if a human is not a human through action. If someone is hopeful for the vision of Allah Almighty, then he must perform deeds. Because the Lord, Exalted and Majestic, has said: "Whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord, let him do righteous deeds."

It became known that a laborer cannot be given wages, nor can any honor remain, if he sits idle. A nation that is not good in action, this is not its own self, it is a mouth to the path. The beginning of the slave mentality starts. Work is praised, a good Muslim is one who works. To wash one's own house with mud, and to go to another's corner, is like a mouth to the path. He has no perfection; trade, industry, craft, and education seem shameful to him. He considers national service a great sin. If a person is found somewhere who understands the nation, and shows the nation that one should not go to the street of others, and kiss the thresholds of strangers, and that kissing feet does not befit the sign of a leader, and this is in reality an insult and disgrace to Islam. And this is a denial of friendship with Allah Almighty. Because Allah Almighty has said: "And whoever among you takes them as allies, then indeed he is one of them." Our capitalist has wealth. God has given him a detailed order separately.
Then the nation laughs at that person, alas! For the state of that person.

Sir Miltin first casts eyes upon his own nation. The carelessness that happens even in the middle of London, I am not aware of it, it is hoped that Sir Miltin will be

Hyde Park, Richmond, and the names of the hotels

He must have heard. It is hoped that he will also be very aware of the night clubs. It is clear that it has become the end of times.

Look at you, the sieve says to the water pot
You have two holes. - Mian Ahmad Shah, Barrister

Poem of Spring

Spring is ready but the nightingale is silent. The flower will come again in the garden, it is not a secret. The water of life lies in the light after the darkness. There is no seeker like Sultan Sikandar. The dead and the living are bowing to the dead. It is not that the lips are of the moon. There is no sad lover like Majnun anywhere. Do not say that that Laila does not exist in the world. That beloved, that beauty, that Joseph of Egypt. There are no buyers like Zulaikha. That melody, that faith, that sign and religion. Alas, that soul does not exist in the world.
Ghulam Muhammad's speech is a pretext for ending the conversation
My heart became a killer, the oath of gold is beyond description

(Ghulam Muhammad of Khushqam - currently Bengal)

Few nations are those that utilize the perfections which God Almighty has bestowed upon man, which in reality is the fulfillment of the covenant that God Almighty has taken from every servant. And we can fulfill this covenant in such a way that we spend these powers through reflection and deliberation in manifesting the properties of the material universe and discovering their inner secrets, and enter the status of the people of understanding from the school of God. "Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day are signs for those of understanding, who reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth." Then we take practical benefit from those properties and correct our worldly state. Then we bow our heads in the presence of our Master, and saying "Our Lord, You have not created this in vain," become worthy of praise and adoration in the court of God. "So remember Me; I will remember you."
From this statement, it became clear that material progress is not contrary to spiritual progress; rather, these two are like sisters. That is, both sides have an inherent requirement: one is a material requirement, and the other is a spiritual requirement. True perfection and real balance in human life are achieved only when a human fulfills all the requirements of nature in a complete manner.
People who emphasize only one requirement of nature can never have a successful life. Secondly, it also became clear that every material progress is linked to spiritual progress.

Dr. Tagore, like Gandhi, is counted among the great people of the world.

It has been a long time since he returned from his world tour. He is old; his age is about seventy years. He has no equal in poetry. He has written many books, for example in English, and at one place in the whole world, he has been ranked number one in poetry, and he has also received an award called the Nobel Prize. In politics, you might have heard of his soft heart. He is now on a tour of India.

He looks at the flower with thorns and collects donations for a college. During his tour of India, he went to Ahmedabad on January 18, 1930, and met Mahatma Gandhi there in his Ashram (school). Meeting with everyone at such a time, when the issue of freedom is lying on the land of India, is very strange.

You may know that Mahatma Gandhi is from Ahmedabad and there is a school there. In the language of India, it is an Ashram. Many boys and girls are educated in that Ashram. So, when he is free from Congress or other national meetings or work, he spends the night in the Ashram. He writes articles for the country's freedom, or gives lessons, or does chores. Whatever he does, he does not do a single penny for himself or for his children and family. His whole time, his thinking, is in this grief, and this is why he is a world-renowned person. The boys and girls of the school also live their lives like him; they do chores, write articles, and do other national work.

Answer: I saw that this Korean youth was seeing me for the first time. I said to him, "O brother! How will you compete and fight against Japan in terms of power and capitalism?"
He replied: "If you divide the people into two groups, one group will be of our own people who will clash with capitalism. The result of this unity and organization will be that we will get rid of Japan. This organization will be among the poor laborers and helpless landowners. And if the people are united, then the special treasury of the country of Japan will also be our country's, and they will join in the war against the capitalists of Japan."
This Mahatma says in one group, the Korean man said, it was not for the wealthy and worldly people, nothing is lost in it. This dream is for the oppressed, laborers, poor landowners, and farmers, who will stand together and bring about a revolution. Every effort in it is true, what hope is there from it? He says to me: "In doubt - I am telling the absolute truth, I am saying it completely correctly."
Dr. Tagore: - The Korean youth said that we people cannot fight against the capitalists along with their (capital). He says it seems at this time that this whole world will fight on the side of the war. These heartfelt thoughts may have been taken from the Russians. The whole world is in a group against the Russians. And for their opposition, no matter how big or great we make, the confrontation of their ideas is nothing. Mahatma Gandhi: - "I think day and night - and I am immersed in deep thought - but there is darkness all around me - and the light has not yet appeared to me - I tell the truth that the goal of life is complete freedom - we do not want Dominion status - the meaning that some people take from this - that is from the British - and they mean something else."

Sir Jagadish Chandra (Bose) has told me that we pay eighty crore rupees in interest annually - I do not understand that we do not have enough to eat - so why do we pay so much money in interest - along with that he also told me that the balance of India's trade is declining - meaning India is lagging behind in trade with other countries - I do not understand that despite good trade, we are becoming poorer every day.
The point is that the debt taken for us is not for our benefit - for such a big, poor and miserable country, there is only a handful of water for one person - so this does not suit its thirty crore population - this does not mean that everyone has their own work and business and money to spend - this means that every idle day, idle hour or moment that passes for you, millions of people of poor India are doing hard labor and heavy work on their backs.
For us millions of educated, helpless, penniless and miserable people, there is no easier or greater source of income than agriculture (farming), we have not even thought about its improvement, we have no capital, we have no education, our average land per person is two acres and even that, like our poor Pashtuns in lakhs of places, is owned by landlords, the big recommendations of these agricultural commissions have no benefit for the helpless farmers who intend to improve agriculture.

In 1890, the Iranian government's granting of the tobacco monopoly to a British company created a very wide field for Sayyid Sahib. To take advantage of this time, Sayyid Sahib sent a famous letter to the supreme Mujtahid of the time, Mirza Hassan Shirazi, informing him of the creation of a document against the Iranian government and the waste and extravagance of the common people's wealth for the benefit of the enemies of Islam. In that letter, he wrote: That until now, the profits made on the lines have confirmed the right to increase in the economy. And granting the monopoly of tobacco has surrendered all of Iran to the hands of Europe. This letter of Sayyid Sahib, which was sent to the clerics, had a great impact.

Here, by the Armenian, he meant the one whose name is Jamal al-Din al-Afghani. There was no one else except that Armenian.
The residents did not believe it at first, and they were also struck with fear, but Ali Asghar Khan insisted. And he gave advice for an honest inspection. 120.right:
Pashtun
Volume 2
June 1939
Bacha Khan's Speech
Dear and respected ones! You are aware of the state of our homeland due to the British colonial system. They have seized our wealth, and our people live in poverty and misery. My heart's desire is that we must become united and struggle against this oppression. They are trying by all means to sow discord among us. But we must understand that our benefit lies in unity. If we do not unite, they will continue to play with our destiny. Today, I call upon you to come and join hands for the freedom of our homeland. We have made many sacrifices in the past years, but these sacrifices are only valuable if we reach our goal. For this, we must believe in ourselves and stand up for our rights. This struggle is not easy, but if we remain steadfast on the path of truth, then success is certain.

Regarding service to Pashtun:
Those who have knowledge of the mentioned field, it is necessary for them to enlighten others about it as well. Just as we have made efforts in the past days to prevent the enemy, it is necessary to carry out our actions for the prosperity of the homeland. Today, our homeland needs every individual to work according to their ability for progress. The measures taken by the British government in Pashtunkhwa are meant to deprive the people of their rights. We must protect ourselves from such conspiracies and become one hand for our national interests.

The owner of that field is He,
The owner of that field is He,

The people are His remembrance,

The world is His earth.

127.right:
Like murder

Regarding the true civilization and education, it is a great necessity for the well-being of the Pashtun and the Pashtun himself that steps be taken to establish a system at the national level so that a broad and standard education system can be created for the people of the country. This is a path in which every citizen of the country can obtain their right. For the importance of this education, it is necessary that efforts be made to create a curriculum at the national level that will enhance the mental abilities of the people. Its goal should be the promotion of knowledge and wisdom for the progress of the homeland, not just for a specific class. The current system that exists pays attention to the interests of a specific class, due to which many members of our society are left without education. We must try to ensure that education is in accordance with the needs of the people and that every citizen can benefit from the blessing of education in their own country. No matter how many obstacles there are in this path, we must move forward for a bright future and work for the progress of our country in the light of knowledge. Our goal should not just be building schools, but creating an environment in which everyone has an equal opportunity to learn. With this, every individual in our society will participate in the progress of the country and a strong society will be created.
127.left:
A short note on the great need for the development of true civilization and education:

In reality, national education is an essential step for the common interests of all and the well-being of society. Unfortunately, the current system in the field of education has a series of shortcomings, due to which the people of the country remain deprived of their true right. In these circumstances, a major change is needed in the field of education that opens the doors of knowledge for every citizen of the country. Current education is for a limited circle, while education as a social right should reach everyone. For the expansion of education, it is necessary that policies be formulated at the government level that take into account the needs of the people and that practical steps be taken for the progress of the country. In this way, the people of our society will not only be equipped with the light of knowledge, but will shine in the world as a strong and developed society. It is the duty of every Pashtun to strive for the popularization of education and to raise their voice for this important cause.

It was midnight and it became the night of tigers. God made me wish that someone would come and take care of me. It was God's work that at three o'clock in the night something like a light appeared in the room from the passage. I wondered who it was. In a very short time, three people came. One of them said that he definitely knows me. I told him that I do not know you. When I said that, he specifically said that I am a friend of someone you know. At this time, I breathed a sigh of relief and was hoping to see what would happen. They didn't ask me what crime are you here for? I told him that I am innocent. They said that you are imprisoned here for a political crime, but the fact is that you are imprisoned here for the death penalty. When I heard this, such sadness came over me that I wished to die. They said that we have received an order to release us from someone who knows you. I said how can I be free? They said you should go with us. I went with them. We encountered many problems on the way, but at last we reached a place where behind a big door we were unblocked and we secretly made our way.
139.left:
Pashtun Atanzi 39 

Before this was the case in Peshawar, there were rains that filled everywhere with water, and orders came at night that any houses in the southern part of Peshawar should be evacuated at this time. People were very afraid because of the increase of this water. At this time, we went there to help them to save their lives. At night there were people's cries, and when we came to their aid, the condition of the people was indescribable. Children screaming, women crying, and men raising their hands for help, it was a heart-rending sight. We tried to find a safe place for them. When we reached a place, there was an old school in Peshawar that we used as a shelter for the refugees. Crowds of people took refuge in this place. We made arrangements for them to eat and drink, and thus saved them from the threat of death. It was an experience that will never be forgotten. The joy on their faces, seeing our help, gave us a great sense of satisfaction.

153.right:
Pashtun Elementary

A look at Albania

Albania's (late) King "Ahmet Zogu" is one of those figures of the world who rose above the ordinary status of his merit and courage.

This king's story is the story of the death of Jalal Beg, among the tribes of Albania. Besides the status of his own business, he was the trusted deputy of Sultan Abdul Hamid. He was born in 1895. How Ahmet Zogu was raised in Albania is a story in itself. Until 1915, Albania was part of the Turkish government. But when a freedom movement started against the Sultan's faulty government in Turkey, the idea of freedom for his own country began in Ahmet Zogu's heart. At the age of twenty, he joined the movement. He endured exile, imprisonment, and the hardships of war. Finally, he succeeded in his goal. In 1925, he became the Prime Minister of Albania and in 1925, he became the head of Albania. Finally, in 1928, due to his ability and power, he was made King of Albania, and he took the title "Zogu".

During the time of the freedom movement, he faced many enemies. The people of Albania, like the Wazirs, believed in blood feuds. Controlling these bloodthirsty people was not an easy task. Ahmet Zogu was a brave man. He controlled them with an iron hand. He was targeted by enemies. There were reports of three hundred (300) attempts to assassinate him. Many attempts were made on his life, but fate saved him, and he remained alive.

He kept his palace under the protection of machine guns and often did not participate in public meetings.

Ahmet Zogu was a very learned man. He could speak German, French, Turkish, and Serbian. He was fond of studying history and spent his lonely nights busy with work for the betterment of the country.

Zogu was very skilled in maintaining law and order among the people. He brought his enemies under control through his good behavior. Whenever he found out that someone intended to rebel, he would invite them to the palace and bring them under control through his good behavior and sweet speech.

A priest, Fan Noli, was his very staunch enemy. When Zogu came to power, he exiled him. He lived in America for a long time. Finally, he became seriously ill, and for his treatment, financial ---

Patriotism is poetry, and poetry is patriotism. In these two, the spirit of Afghan patriotism is woven into poetry in such a way that every flower seems appropriate for the country's meadows. Wherever Khushal Khan's poetry is discussed, this patriotism is also discussed on appropriate occasions.
Khushal's war poetry - its usage, rhythm, and rhyme stir the heart of every Pashtun. And it stands as a monument over the history of Pashto. These poets wrote that the war of a Pashtun against a Pashtun and the hope of a Pashtun war can be seen. And the thoughts of his wars are the judges. In these great volumes of the era, the theory is that the merits and beauties of these poets are all present. It is a true mirror of Pashtun patriotic poetry. Because it is a reflection of the Pashtun's simple, sharp, and warrior nature and their national state. In this Diwan, the hardships of the Pashtun's life are depicted in every home. In every village, the Pashtun war, their internal conflicts, the oppression of the Khans, the expansion of oil, the instilling of fear, the Pashtun's battles with the Mughals, the destruction of the Pashtun land, the habits and customs of the people, their way of life, bravery, hospitality, and the standards of other civilization and culture, and the identity of the Afghan, moral standards, and warfare.

Poetic descriptions of music. Poetic study of natural laughter. Ghazals of love and affection. Rubaiyat and qita'at in chronograms, etc., poets and myself in this style.
