165.left:
Pashtun Utmanzai
21 June 1939
Volume (5) Number (14)
A message of condolence from Pashtun
The struggle of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan for the service of the nation is an example that will always be a torch for future generations. He spent a large part of his life for the unity of the Pashtuns and for winning their rights. In his death, not only his family but the entire Pashtun nation is in grief. Remembering his services and following his path is the duty of every Pashtun. We pray for his forgiveness in the court of God. May Allah Almighty grant patience to his family and all his friends. Amen.

166.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi
21 June 1939
A Pashtun's message of grief on the death of Khan Sahib God Dad Khan
It is a pity that a great leader like Khan Sahib God Dad Khan, who understood the pain and sorrow of the nation's heart and a mujahid for the freedom of the country, who always raised his voice against the oppression and tyranny of the British, is not among us today. His death is a great tragedy for the entire nation. In this grief, not only his family and friends, but all those who dreamed of the independence and prosperity of the country, are in fear and mourning. God Dad Khan Sahib's life and struggle history is a clear example for those who are ready to sacrifice their lives in the path of truth. With his death, a page of the struggle of Pakhtunkhwa was turned. May God have mercy on him and may heaven be his place. Our condolences to his family and the rest of the family.
166.left:
A message of sympathy for the Pashtun
The struggle of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan for the service of the nation is an example that will always be a torch for future generations. He spent a large part of his life for the unity of the Pashtuns and for winning their rights. In his death, not only his family but the entire Pashtun nation is in grief. Remembering his services and following his path is the duty of every Pashtun. We pray for his forgiveness in the court of God. May Allah Almighty grant his family and all his friends the patience to bear this loss. Amen.

167.right:
Introduction to the subject

Acquaintance for the sake of a goal is all for a purpose; there is no acquaintance without a purpose. If the goal is not achieved, the acquaintance does not remain; if the goal is achieved, the acquaintance does not last until morning. God's works are strange; there was a time when the British and the Russians were bitter enemies. Each side spoke ill of the other's work; in their opinion, the Russians were infidels. And regarding the British infidels, they had spread the notion that there were no greater oppressors in the world than them. As time passed, the British were not recognized as their opponents. The wise and knowledgeable would remain silent for a purpose, but such is their situation. It is known that the Bolsheviks were presenting a very bad map of the world. In India, the British power was sufficient for them, as they considered all of India to be under their influence. And as much as they had among them, there it was recognized among the Pashtuns. Whatever happened in their own country, they committed great oppression with Russia. Whoever understands them knows they were not of the province; in the words of Europe, only the British and Russians had taken the lead. Their efforts were together.

Among the Pashtuns on the border, they started their propaganda. May God make their believers easy. May God make them go away. They would approach the Red government with trickery. They forgot the British infidels. Regarding the rebellion, they had first taken the path of propaganda. Socialism and the desire for socialism were a deception for the Pashtun nation. It was the work of the British government, and the Bolsheviks were not to be blamed for it.

The sincerity of Abdul Ghaffar Khan is evident in his commitment to the principles of Islam, seeking paradise rather than the torment of hell.  Ignorance is a barrier to understanding, and the pursuit of knowledge is essential.  The practice of unjustly killing believers is abhorrent, and the protection of the faithful from oppression is paramount.  Love for one’s fellow Muslim is a virtue, and rivalry in the arena should be avoided.  Let us cast off the yoke of slavery and embrace the freedom granted by the Prophet.  May the heart harbor the hope of the Holy Prophet’s intercession.  Let the Qur’an be our guide, and may we follow the example of the Commander of the Faithful, Abdul Ghaffar Khan.
(Al-Abd Ghulam Haider Sahib – Head of the Leaders)

---
India and the Frontier Regions

Efforts to improve the administration of the Indian frontier regions have yielded limited results, as there is little prospect of achieving true independence for the people of the region.  To combat the persistence of slavery and the practice of forced marriage, it is essential to address the underlying causes of these issues.  Therefore, it is necessary to provide young men with opportunities for education and employment, to discourage the continuation of slavery and forced marriage, and to advance the cause of freedom through these means.  Let us strive towards this goal, providing the young generation with the opportunity to inherit the legacy of their ancestors, to claim their own destiny, and to build a future free from oppression.

203.right:
21 July 1921

Frontier News

Official inauguration of the sugar factory - On 16 July, Honorable Doctor Khan, Prime Minister of the Frontier, inaugurated the sugar factory in Takht-i-Bahi, Mardan district.
On this occasion, government officers and local Khans were present.
After the inauguration, the Prime Minister delivered a brief speech and stated: This factory
was started with four lakh rupees, out of which two lakh rupees
have been contributed by the Frontier government.

Grand Political Conference at Marghuz - On 19 and 20 July
a grand political conference was held in Marghuz, Mardan district, on the weekend
under the presidency of Hussain Ahmad Madani. The purpose of which
was announced after careful consideration. The president of the reception committee, Khan
Abdul Aziz Khan, M.L.A., read a brief
welcome address. Following this, the honorable president of the conference delivered that grand
historical presidential address, every word of which was related to Islam
and national politics. Both sides of the gathering were addressed by the provincial
leader Ghani Afghan Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, who shed light on the Khudai Khidmatgar
movement and other current affairs. In this
gathering, besides thousands of Khudai Khidmatgars, countless people were present.

22
21 July 1939
British Policy
The acceptance of humility and humiliation in the face of the powerful, and arrogance and violence in the face of weak nations, is clearly explained by past events. During the British occupation of the Rhineland and the Treaty of Sèvres, Italy occupied Abyssinia, and regarding Japan, Germany made an understanding with the Soviet Union contrary to the Anti-Comintern Pact. In the joint Italian and German involvement in the Spanish Civil War, the British government's stance was evident. Following the defeat of the republican government, all the despicable actions of the Franco government are akin to poisonous stings. Japan's blatant humiliation of the British in China is a testament to Britain's decline. The British policy of non-interference is apparent to the whole world, yet Britain has accepted every instance of humiliation and has stood by watching every kind of tragedy.
Now, instead of Japan, in the areas of intervention, Great Britain will show whether it will choose honor or humiliation; this government's humility in accepting this humiliation is apparent to the newspaper columnists of the government. Twelve pieces of advice from Japanese mothers to their daughters at the time of marriage

(1) After marriage, do not consider your mother-in-law as anything other than your own mother. In fact, consider your mother-in-law as your own mother, consider her as your own, and in any situation, do not upset her.
(2) After marriage, when you enter someone's land, consider obedience to them as your duty.
(3) Try not to give your husband a chance to complain.
(4) Do not hold any kind of grudge or enmity with the people of the house. This will ruin your life.
(5) If your husband speaks harshly about something, do not argue back. Instead, try to calm his anger.
(6) Maintain good behavior with your husband's relatives. Do not interfere in every matter or his personal affairs.
(7) Wake up early in the morning and stay awake until late at night. And whatever has happened during the day, do not share it in any gathering.
(8) Manage the household affairs with careful thought. Make it your own habit.
(9) Do not tell the West about the state of Japanese upbringing and the condition of the home.

Advice to the student
(Pashto Master, Mannan School, Bannu)
1- Boy! Pursue your interest in Pashto with the intention of completing your education.
2- Do not let yourself make mistakes, or you will lead a purposeless life tomorrow.
3- Understanding everything is necessary; the decline of life and wealth lies in ignorance. If you do not understand this, you will remain deprived of the world for a long time.
4- Seek knowledge and art; if you act well in your own time, you will be blessed with the fortune of this world and the hereafter.
5- Use knowledge and wisdom in every task, for success lies in this.
6- Read literature in your books, as it is also necessary in education.
7- Have a passion for studying beautiful books so that you may understand knowledge and art.
8- Follow the path of Pashto in your work so that you may maintain your place in the world.
9- Now you know what is needed for Pashto education; serve your country in the light of knowledge.
10- Show your skill in everything and see what is good for your life; take the path of effort for your life.

214.right:
The attitude of Muslims towards the enemies of Islam

The history of Islam's attitude and behavior towards the enemies is preserved, what kind of attitude the Muslims had towards the enemies, and what sufferings the Muslims suffered from them, its history is recorded in a safe place. 

The holy religion of Islam, at the beginning of Islam, what kind of oppression was done by the infidels against the master of Islam, Muhammad, peace be upon him, and his companions, on the one hand, with their own lives, and against the infidels, when they were in Makkah, what kind of oppression was done on their lives in a special way, at the same time, what kind of idolatry the people against them were in, so the Prophet (PBUH) always told them about religion. As the owner of Islam in the way of Da'wah and Truth, when they were in war with the infidels and what was their attitude towards them in accordance with the commands of Allah, there is a clear example of the greatness of the holy religion of Islam and its teachings. When the Muslims in Madinah faced the infidels, they always chose their way with patience and tolerance in the face of oppression. Against the enemies, they always acted according to the teachings of Islam. Throughout the history of Islam, the attitude of the Muslims towards the enemies was such that whenever the atrocities of the enemies increased, they would stand firm against their atrocities with patience and tolerance. The stories of their patience and tolerance are preserved in the pages of history. How they treated their enemies according to the teachings of Islam was a sign of their faith and piety. In many cases, in contrast to the gentleness and humbleness of the Muslims, the cruelties and wrongdoings of the enemies have been mentioned very carefully in various parts of history, how the Muslims stood firm for the supremacy of Islam at the time of all kinds of difficulties.
214.left:
In this period of Islamic history, when they were facing infidelity, they lived with great patience. At that time, as an example of enduring the oppression of enemies, when a storm of cruelty and oppression was raised by the infidels against the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) and his companions, the Muslims chose the path of patience and tolerance in response. In the face of them, while explaining the word of Allah Almighty and the truth of Islam, they were busy with their invitation work despite all kinds of difficulties. When the oppression of the infidels against the Muslims in Mecca increased, the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) finally migrated from Mecca to Medina Munawara by the command of Allah Almighty. In Medina, a series of conspiracies and plots by the infidels to destroy them was ongoing, but the followers of Islam had prepared to defend the religion of Islam against all kinds of enemy conspiracies. They fought with a kind of faith and courage to protect the religion of Islam against all kinds of hardships caused by the enemies. Anyway, the stories of the enmity of the infidels at the beginning of Islam are very numerous, but the stories of the patience and tolerance of the Muslims are even more numerous, as to how they sacrificed their lives in the path of their Lord, but did not turn away from the path of Islam. In Medina, a conspiratorial group of enemies of Islam, led by Abdullah ibn Ubayy ibn Salul, always tried to create a rift in the ranks of Islam, but the Muslims chose the path of unity and solidarity against these conspiracies of the enemies, and they failed their conspiracies with great patience.

235.right: The people of Peshawar declare their desire for freedom. They believe that the moral standards of their teachers and elders are paramount, and that these principles should guide their actions. They urge the government to recognize the importance of education and to support schools, ensuring that future generations receive a proper education.  They express a hope that the government will prioritize the welfare of the people and strive for a just and prosperous future, recognizing the need to address the grievances of the populace and to foster unity and stability within the nation.  They pray that freedom will be established in India, and that figures like Maulana Abdul Mannan, who champion the cause of liberty, will continue to inspire the people.  This declaration, issued on August 1939, reaffirms the commitment of the people of Peshawar to the pursuit of freedom and justice.

Definition of Simile: It is when two things are compared in a specific quality
to indicate a relationship, it is called (Simile), and among those two things, the first
is called (Mushabbah) and the second is called (Mushabbah bihi), and that specific meaning
which is common between both is called (Wajh-e-Shabah), like Zaid in bravery
is like a lion - in this example, Zaid and the lion share the quality of bravery

Allama Taftazani, in the book named Mutawwal, which is divided into two parts, or used for verbal and semantic purposes, uses these in speech as a form of embellishment, not every one of which is called Ilm-e-Badi. There are two types of Ilm-e-Badi. One is verbal (1) and the other is semantic arts (Sana'i-e-Ma'nawi). One of the types of semantic science is the art of Tibaq (antithesis). I leave the others to the imagination of the aforementioned mentioned above. The art of Tibaq is also called so in books of rhetoric. And this is an art in which two meanings or contrasts are mentioned in speech. This art has three types: (1) Nominal Tibaq, (2) Verbal Tibaq, (3) Particle Tibaq. The type occurs in the form of affirmation or in the form of negation. (1) An example of nominal Tibaq is "Wa tahsabuhum ayqazan wahum ruqud" (and you would think them awake, while they were asleep). Note of the meeting of the Municipal Committee Peshawar, dated August 11
Under Section 116 of the Punjab Municipal Act 1890, Section 161, a public notice is given that the Municipal Committee Peshawar has proposed taxes for one year at the following rates, and this committee is imposing octroi duty for one year on the following items.
Lease of third-grade petrol, per gallon 0-3
Lease of third-grade kerosene oil 0-3
For the same account, two paise per two paise

If there is any objection regarding this proposal, it should be submitted in writing by the person himself within a period of 30 days from this date to the Secretary of the Municipal Committee. The Municipal Committee will consider the objections received on this proposal, and the proposal will then be sent again to the local government authority for approval.

Secretary, Municipal Committee Peshawar, M.A.L.B.
